skill.name: data-analysis
skill.description: Guidance for analyzing Empathy Ledger transcripts, themes, quotes, story suggestions, Supabase queries, and analytics views.
skill.summary: Analyze Empathy Ledger transcript themes, quotes, suggestions, and analytics patterns.
skill.author: Acurioustractor

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, credential, crypto, network, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found they are primarily markdown examples and domain vocabulary, not executable malicious behavior. The real residual risk is medium because the skill recommends external AI analysis of transcript content and includes broad query patterns that require consent, tenancy, and publication controls. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: External AI processing of sensitive transcript content
medium_findings.description: The skill documents sending transcript_content to an Anthropic analysis call and recommends resolving OpenAI quota for remaining transcript analysis. Storytelling transcripts and cultural elements can contain sensitive personal or cultural information, so implementations need consent, minimization, and provider data handling controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation clearly shows transcript content being sent to an external AI SDK and describes pending batch analysis. The skill is not malicious, but the privacy risk is real if implemented without controls.

medium_findings.title: Examples require consistent tenant and publication controls
medium_findings.description: Several query examples demonstrate theme, quote, and search access patterns. Some examples include tenant or published filters, but generic examples using broad selects must be adapted carefully to avoid exposing draft, private, or cross-tenant content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The concern depends on how examples are copied into the host application. The documentation is legitimate, but broad query examples need access-control review before production use.

low_findings.title: Static command execution findings are documentation examples
low_findings.description: The external command findings occur in fenced markdown examples for TypeScript, SQL, TSX, and bash. The skill contains guidance text and examples, not executable Ruby backtick calls or install scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is inside markdown code fences or tables. I found no runnable skill script, hook, or instruction that executes these commands automatically.

low_findings.title: Credential and weak-crypto alerts are domain term matches
low_findings.description: The sensitive and weak cryptography alerts are caused by domain terms such as key_quotes, featuredQuote, theme keys, and identity themes. No certificate file, private key, hash routine, or cryptographic operation is present in the reviewed content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding lines show application data fields and taxonomy labels. There is no evidence of stored credentials or weak cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance and network alerts are local development examples
low_findings.description: The network and reconnaissance hits are localhost curl examples, status metrics, and database analytics snippets. They do not contact external collection endpoints or enumerate the host environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples target local API endpoints and describe project status. Legitimate development use is clear, and no credential exfiltration endpoint appears.

dangerous_patterns.title: External AI processing of sensitive transcript content
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents sending transcript_content to an Anthropic analysis call and recommends resolving OpenAI quota for remaining transcript analysis. Storytelling transcripts and cultural elements can contain sensitive personal or cultural information, so implementations need consent, minimization, and provider data handling controls.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documentation clearly shows transcript content being sent to an external AI SDK and describes pending batch analysis. The skill is not malicious, but the privacy risk is real if implemented without controls.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent ways to extract themes, quotes, and recommendations from storytelling data. This skill provides reusable patterns for transcript analysis, Supabase queries, and analytics displays.
actual_capabilities: Defines transcript analysis fields for themes, key quotes, summaries, sentiment, and cultural elements.
Shows Supabase array, full-text search, materialized view, and analytics query patterns.
Provides theme taxonomy, theme aliases, color mappings, and hierarchy helpers.
Documents story suggestion logic based on shared themes and overlap scores.
Maps analysis data to story cards, search results, dashboards, and storyteller profiles.
Includes sync status guidance for batch analysis scripts and API endpoints.
limitations: This skill is documentation only and does not execute analysis by itself.
Examples assume an Empathy Ledger schema with transcripts, stories, storytellers, and theme fields.
External AI processing requires consent, privacy review, and provider configuration before use.
SQL examples need validation against the target database schema and access policies.

use_cases.title: Standardize transcript insights
use_cases.description: Use shared fields and patterns when adding AI summaries, themes, quotes, and analysis status to transcript workflows.

use_cases.title: Build story discovery features
use_cases.description: Use theme overlap, quote display, and suggestion examples to create related story and search experiences.

use_cases.title: Design analytics queries
use_cases.description: Use Supabase query examples to report theme frequency, quote counts, storyteller expertise, and content trends.

prompt_templates.title: Find Relevant Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the data-analysis skill to identify the best pattern for adding theme badges to story cards. Summarize the files and fields I should use.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Transcript Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the data-analysis skill to plan transcript analysis for themes, quotes, summaries, and sentiment. Include privacy checks and storage fields.

prompt_templates.title: Review Supabase Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the data-analysis skill to review my Supabase query for theme matching. Check performance, tenancy filtering, and result limits.

prompt_templates.title: Design Analytics Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the data-analysis skill to design an analytics workflow from transcript analysis through materialized views, UI components, and batch sync operations.

output_examples.input: Add related story recommendations to a story detail page.
output: Use the current story themes as the source set.
Query published stories from the same tenant and exclude the current story.
Rank candidates by shared themes and show a short reason for each recommendation.

output_examples.input: Create a dashboard for theme trends.
output: Aggregate published story themes by month and theme name.
Use an indexed view when the dataset becomes large.
Display counts, top themes, and storyteller coverage with clear filters.
Confirm consent and data handling rules before sending transcript content to a provider.
Store extracted themes, quotes, summaries, and processing status in transcript records.
Keep tenant filters and access controls on every read and write path.

best_practices: Filter every story, transcript, and analytics query by tenant or publication scope before returning results.
Validate AI output before storing themes, quotes, sentiment, or summary fields.
Use database indexes and materialized views for repeated theme and quote analytics.
anti_patterns: Sending full transcripts to external AI services without consent or data handling review.
Fetching all story columns and filtering large datasets only in the client.
Mixing draft, private, and published content in public suggestion or analytics results.

faq.question: What kind of data does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on transcripts, stories, storytellers, themes, quotes, summaries, suggestions, and analytics tables.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands or connect to a database?
faq.answer: No. It is markdown guidance with example commands and queries that a developer may adapt.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why does the audit mention external AI risk?
faq.answer: The examples show transcript content being sent to an AI provider, which requires privacy and consent controls.

faq.question: Are the Supabase queries ready to paste into production?
faq.answer: No. Validate schema names, tenant filters, indexes, and access policies before production use.

faq.question: What should teams review before publishing derived insights?
faq.answer: Review consent, cultural sensitivity, quote accuracy, publication status, and access controls for the source material.
